nativeRange.habitat: Streams and slow-moving freshwater bodies on the island of Sumatra, where it grows along shaded stream margins and can experience seasonal emersed/submerged transitions.

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A Sumatran rosette crypt with long, lanceolate to arrowhead-shaped olive-green leaves. Generally hardy and beginner-friendly; well suited to midground or background placement in low-to-moderate-light tanks.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne pontederiifolia is a Sumatran rosette plant in the family Araceae, generally reaching about 10-25 cm tall in the aquarium with long lanceolate to arrowhead/heart-shaped leaves that are usually olive-green (occasionally with pink-tinged undersides under stronger light). It is widely considered beginner-friendly: it tolerates low to moderate lighting, does not require CO2 injection (though it responds well to it with faster growth and more compact, richly coloured leaves), and accepts a wide pH range (sources cite ~6.0-7.5) with soft to moderately hard water. As with all crypts, it is a heavy root feeder — plant it in nutrient-rich aquarium soil and supplement with root tabs near the root zone for best results. Plant by burying the roots up to (but NOT including) the crown where the leaves emerge from the rhizome; burying the crown causes rot. CRYPT MELT WARNING: like other Cryptocoryne species, C. pontederiifolia is prone to crypt melt when newly planted, after a water-change shock, or after any major parameter shift — the plant may shed most or all of its existing leaves over a few days. This is a normal acclimation response; do NOT pull up the rhizome and roots, as the plant typically regrows new leaves adapted to the new conditions within 2-4 weeks. To minimize melt, keep parameters stable and avoid moving an established plant once it has settled. Native-range note: Modern Aquarium and the established taxonomic literature identify this species as a Sumatran endemic; Buce Plant lists 'Borneo,' which appears to be an error — we have used the Sumatran origin. Temperature range 22-28 °C reflects the consensus across Modern Aquarium (68-82 °F = 20-28 °C) and Aquarium Lesson (22-25 °C), trimmed to the typical aquarium target.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plants alongside the parent; once a daughter plant has developed several leaves and a healthy root system, it can be separated from the runner and replanted. Rhizome division is also possible: cut a healthy rhizome into sections, each retaining leaves and roots, and replant. Like other crypts, C. pontederiifolia can flower (producing the spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirmed Sumatran origin, lanceolate olive-green leaves, moderate lighting requirement, midground-to-background placement, nutrient-rich-substrate preference, melt sensitivity under unstable parameters, and temperature range 68-82 °F (20-28 °C).

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Araceae), pH 6-7.5, slow growth rate, low-to-medium lighting, midground-to-background placement, easy care level, quality-aquarium-soil recommendation, optional CO2, and propagation by rhizome cutting/splitting. Lists 'Borneo' as native range, which conflicts with Modern Aquarium and the established taxonomic record for this Sumatran species; we have used Sumatra.

additional.name: Aquarium Lesson
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Araceae), temperature 22-25 °C, pH 6-7.5, kH 2-14 dKH, low light demand, slow growth, rosette form, midground-to-background placement, easy care level, and propagation by daughter plants/runners.
